The stock was down as much as 11.5% in premarket trading, after the software and cloud services company reported late Monday fiscal first-quarter revenue that missed expectations and provided a downbeat second-quarter outlook.

title: U.S. stocks open lower as tech shares retreat after Oracle earnings; investors await Apple event, inflation data
text: U.S. stock indexes opened lower on Tuesday after starting the week on a strong footing, with disappointing guidance from Oracle weighing on technology stocks. The Nasdaq Composite COMP, -0.34% was off 0.4%, while the S&P 500 SPX, -0.25% was dropping 0.3% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA, -0.15% was falling 0.2%.

title: Oracle's stock dives toward worst post-earnings performance in 10 years
text: Shares of Oracle Corp. ORCL, +0.31% tanked 9.5% early Tuesday, enough to pace the S&P 500's SPX, +0.67% premarket decliners, in the wake of the business software giant's disappointing earnings report and revenue guidance. The stock's selloff, which comes after it closed Monday at a record $126.71, is on track to be the biggest one-day post-earnings performance since March 21, 2013, when the stock dropped 9.7% after the company reported fiscal third-quarter results.
